title: Multi-user retail photo product creation system and method of use
abstract: Described herein is a multi-user retail photo kiosk system that includes multiple touch screen and Graphic User Interface (GUI) controlled consumer photo editing, photo print and product selection, and photo order placement terminals and multiple photo printers. The user photo kiosk terminals can receive images. Billing and payment is accomplished using barcoded receipts provided at the user terminal for payments for purchased photo products. A printer array incorporating various printers capable of producing prints or various sizes and formats can be configured as a horizontal counter or as a vertical tower and includes means to alert the user that their photo print order is complete. In addition, the system automatically notifies operators if equipment service, maintenance, media refilling, and/or post printing finishing procedures are required.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  We claim: 1. A method of operating a photo product creation system, comprising: receiving, via one or more user-accessible image input devices at a user input terminal, one or more user-provided images; receiving, at the user input terminal, a photo order, the photo order comprising one or more user selected photo products; transmitting the user-provided images and the photo order from the user input terminal to one or more central computer processors; routing the photo order from the one or more central computer processors to one or more output devices configured to produce each of the user selected photo products in the photo order; calculating a fee for the photo order; calculating an approximate time of completion each of the user selected photo products in the photo order; producing a receipt at the user terminal, the receipt comprising an identification of the photo order; displaying, at a user-accessible output array unit, an order status indicator, the order status indicator comprising the approximate time of completion for each of the user selected photo products and a pick-up location for each of the user selected photo products; transmitting, by the one or more central computer processors, a notification to a remote communication device whether any of the user selected photo products requires specialty finishing; and updating, at the user-accessible output array unit, the order status indicator upon completion of all of the user selected photo products in the photo order. 2. The method of claim 1, further comprising receiving, at the user input terminal, user-selected photo edits. 3. The method of claim 1, wherein the one or more user-accessible image input devices are selected from the group consisting of: a hardcopy scanner, a disk reader, 

 a memory card reader, a USB tether, a camera, a wireless Bluetooth communication device, a wireless WIFI communication device, and a modem to access remotely stored images via a communications network. 4. The method of claim 1, wherein the receipt is either a physical receipt or virtual receipt, the receipt further comprising alphanumeric and machine readable information representing the requested photo order. 5. The method of claim 1, wherein the receipt is a virtual receipt and the method further comprises transmitting the virtual receipt to a remote communication device. 6. The method of claim 1, wherein the one or more output devices are selected from the group consisting of: a thermal dye transfer printer, an inkjet printer, an electrophotographic printer, a disk writer, a portable memory device, and a modem configured to transfer digital images. 7. The method of claim 1, wherein the user input terminal comprises a touch screen display, a Graphic User Interface (GUI) configured to receive user-input controls, voice input means, digital tablet input means, pointing device input means, gesture input means. 8. The method of claim 1, further comprising receiving one or more user- provided images of the group consisting of: a hardcopy print, a negative, a conventional digital image. 9. The method of claim 8, further comprising receiving one or more user- provided images via a camera integrated into the user input terminal. 10. The method of claim 1, wherein the output devices comprise operator- accessible devices and user-accessible devices. 11. The method of claim 1, further comprising reading the receipt, at the user- accessible output array unit, to complete the transaction. 12. The method of claim 1, further comprising producing one or more of the user selected photo products on a specialty output device. 13. The method of claim 1, further comprising performing finishing procedures to complete one or more of the user selected photo products, the finishing procedures selected from the group consisting of: drying, folding, creasing, scoring, cutting, stamping, binding, coating, rolling, mounting, framing, matting, image transfer, and packaging. 14. The method of claim 1, further comprising producing a notification on the remote communication device, the notification selected from the group consisting of: a text message, an audio message, and an audio visual presentation. 15. The method of claim 1, further comprising transmitting, from the one or more central computer processors to the remote communication device, task information relevant to the photo order, the task information selected from the group consisting of: output device user instructions, output device maintenance procedures, photo product finishing instructions, and third party contact information. 16. The method of claim 1, wherein the remote communication device is selected from the group consisting of: a pager, a smart phone, a remote display terminal, and a display equipped check out terminal. 17. The method of claim 15, wherein the output device user instructions comprise instructions for turning on a first output device and for readying the first output device. 18. The method of claim 15, wherein the photo product finishing instructions comprise instructions for preparing photo finishing materials and for readying one or more additional photo finishing equipment units. 19. The method of claim 1, wherein the user-accessible output array unit comprises a master display, and the method further comprises displaying, on the master display, the estimated completion time and retrieval location for one or more users to retrieve their photo product order. 20. The method of claim 19, further comprising replicating the master display interface on the remote communication device, a remote display terminal, or a display equipped checkout terminal. 21. A photo product creation system, comprising: a user input terminals; a central computer processor, a user-accessible output array unit comprising a master display and at least one printer bay, each printer bay comprising an output device; and an operator-accessible finishing station; wherein the user input terminal, the central computer processor, the user- accessible output array unit, and the operator-accessible finishing station are operatively interconnected and configured to communicate. 22. The system of claim 21, wherein each printer bay further comprises an output collection bin and the user-accessible output array unit further comprises, for each printer bay, an indicator comprising at least one printer bay code selected from the group consisting of: an alphanumeric human readable message, a machine-readable code, a color code, a Near Field Communication device, a Radio Frequency Communication device, and an illuminated indicator. 23. The system of claim 22, wherein each of the output collection bins comprises a means to detect when an output device has completed producing prints and when the completed prints have been deposited into the output collection bin. 24. The system of claim 22, wherein each of the output collection bins comprises a means to detect when a set of completed prints has been retrieved from the output collection bin. 25. The system of claim 23, wherein the means to detect when an output device has completed producing prints is selected from the group consisting of: a motion detector, a light beam emitter and detector pair, an electromechanical switch, and an electromagnetic switch. 26. The system of claim 23, wherein the means to detect when an output device has completed producing prints is configured to communicate to the central computer processor when and printing has completed. 27. The system of claim 23, wherein the means to detect when completed prints have been retrieved is configured to communicate to the central computer processor when the prints are retrieved. 28. The system of claim 21, wherein the master display is configured to display an estimated completion time and a retrieval location for a user-initiated photo order. 29. The system of claim 21, wherein the operator-accessible finishing station comprises a specialty output device, a photo finishing equipment unit, and an indicator for each of the specialty output device and the photo finishing equipment unit, the indicator comprising a communication means selected from the group consisting of: an alphanumeric human readable message, a machine-readable code, a color code, a Near Field Communication device, a Radio Frequency Communication device, an illuminated indicator, and/or a display. 30. The system of claim 29, further comprising a remote communication device configured to communicate with at least the indicator for the specialty output device and the indicator for the photo finishing equipment unit.
